EurovisionEntry.title: EurovisionEntry
EurovisionEntry.description: Represents a single Eurovision song entry for a specific year.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EurovisionEntry entity.

year.description: The year the Eurovision song entry participated.

country.description: The country represented by this Eurovision entry.

songTitle.description: The title of the Eurovision song.

videoUrl.description: The URL for the embedded performance video.

description.description: An optional description or background information about the entry, useful for AI insights.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application, primarily for voting purposes. Authentication details are handled externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, provided by the external authentication system.

username.description: A user-friendly username for display purposes.

Vote.title: Vote
Vote.description: Records a user's vote for a specific Eurovision entry in a given year.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vote entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who cast this vote. (Relationship: User 1:N Vote)

eurovisionEntryId.description: Reference to the EurovisionEntry that received this vote. (Relationship: EurovisionEntry 1:N Vote)

year.description: The year for which the vote was cast. Crucial for enforcing 'one vote per user per year' policy.

points.description: The points awarded to the entry (e.g., 1-12 points).

votedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the vote was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Owned by the respective user. Authorization ensures `request.auth.uid` matches `userId` for read/write access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains details for each Eurovision song entry. Documents are publicly readable. Includes denormalized 'totalPoints' and 'voteCount' fields for scoreboard aggregation, updated transactionally from votes. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: entryId
params.description: The unique ID of the Eurovision entry.

definition.description: Records a user's vote for a specific Eurovision entry in a given year. The 'voteIdentifier' is a composite of '{year}_{eurovisionEntryId}' to enforce uniqueness (one vote per user per entry per year). Includes denormalized 'userId', 'eurovisionEntryId', and 'year' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who cast the vote, matching the parent path segment and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: voteIdentifier
params.description: A unique identifier for the vote, structured as '{year}_{eurovisionEntryId}' to ensure one vote per user per entry per year.

definition.description: Serves as a global role collection for administrators. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` grants administrative privileges, following the DBAC pattern. No specific schema beyond the ID is required, but it conceptually refers to a User entity.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is an administrator, matching `request.auth.uid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **EurovisionEntry:** Documents in `/eurovision_entries/{entryId}` are publicly readable. Write access is granted to administrators, whose roles are checked independently via the dedicated `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection. This DBAC (Database Access Control) pattern avoids any `get()` calls on parent documents or the `EurovisionEntry` itself for authorization, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rules.
2.  **User:** User profiles at `/users/{userId}` are inherently authorization-independent. Access rules simply verify `request.auth.uid == userId` for owner-based read/write, requiring no additional `get()` operations.
3.  **Vote:** The `Vote` documents are nested under the user at `/users/{userId}/votes/{voteIdentifier}`. Crucially, each `Vote` document will explicitly contain its `userId`, `eurovisionEntryId`, and `year`. Authorization rules will verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` in the path (`{userId}`) and the `userId` field within the document (`request.resource.data.userId`). This denormalization of the `userId` into the `Vote` document itself ensures that the authorization context is entirely self-contained, eliminating any need for `get()` calls to a parent `User` document.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **EurovisionEntry:** The `/eurovision_entries` collection is designed for efficient and secure `list` operations. Since all entries are publicly browsable, read access is global. For the 'Dynamic Scoreboard' feature, each `EurovisionEntry` document will include denormalized aggregate fields such as `totalPoints` and `voteCount`. These fields are updated transactionally when votes are cast or modified. This structure allows the scoreboard to query and filter directly on the `/eurovision_entries` collection (e.g., by `year`, `totalPoints`), ensuring `list` operations are efficient and do not require filtering out unauthorized data, thus supporting QAPs.
2.  **User:** The `/users/{userId}` path ensures that `list` operations are implicitly restricted to the authenticated user querying their own profile, as the rule `request.auth.uid == userId` will prevent unauthorized broad listing of user data.
3.  **Vote:** The `/users/{userId}/votes/{voteIdentifier}` structure allows users to `list` their *own* votes efficiently. Firestore security rules will ensure that a user can only query their own subcollection, preventing unauthorized `list` operations across all users' private votes. Any global aggregations required for the scoreboard are handled by the denormalized fields in `EurovisionEntry`, not by `list` operations across all `Vote` subcollections, thereby maintaining QAP integrity.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   `EurovisionEntry` documents are grouped in `/eurovision_entries` with a homogeneous security posture (public read, admin write).
*   `User` profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`, using path-based ownership for private data.
*   `Vote` entities are structured hierarchically under the `User` at `/users/{userId}/votes/{voteIdentifier}`. The `voteIdentifier` will be a composite of `{year}_{eurovisionEntryId}` to enforce uniqueness (one vote per user per entry per year) directly through the document ID and simplify rules for creation/updates.
*   Global roles for administrators are handled by the existence of a document in `/roles_admin/{adminId}`, using the efficient DBAC pattern.
